info.title: Accounts
info.description:
![JOF1](https://www.jopacc.com/sites/default/files/2023-12/jpc-internal-jof-banner.png)


## This is the initial draft of the Jordan Open Finance specification

##### The services in this Open API specification are Direct-Call Services (DCS). These services can be accessed by direct calls to the banks or by using an aggregator central hub such as JoPACC. However, the URLs and routes for these services are designed for direct-calls only.

For the Open API specification of the aggregated services, refer to the Aggregator Services specification.

![services](https://www.jopacc.com/sites/default/files/2023-12/jpc-internal-jof-services.jpg)

#### Services in Jordan Open Finance Specification (JOF) are divided into 4 categories based on their content and nature of consent:
### Account Information Services (AIS)
This category covers services that are related to customers' financial accounts. This category has two sub-categories:
  1. **MY AIS**:
    Services that allow an account holder to view their own information. For a TPP to retrieve this information, they require the consent of the user.
  2. **Others AIS**:
    Services that enable a TPP to view the account information of a user who is not their customer and who hasn't initiated the request. These services require the user whose information is being retrieved to have consented, such as the IBAN Confirmation service.
### Payment Initiation Services (PIS)
This category involves services that allow TPPs to initiate payments on behalf of the user. The user's consent is required by the TPP.
### Extended Services (ES)
This category involves services that require the consent of an account-holder but does not relate directly to their account-information. The services under this category extend the functionalities of their account, such as the OTP service.
### Facilities and Products Services (FPS)
This category includes services that specifically provide data about the Financial Institution's facilities and products. It does not contain any information about the customers or require their consent.

#### The Central Bank of Jordan has classified Open Finance  consents into Account Information consent and Payment Initiation consent. The above categories are based on the CBJ's classifications and divide them further for the user's convenience.

Some useful links:
- [About JoPACC](https://jopacc.com)
                  
![JOF2](https://www.jopacc.com/sites/default/files/2023-12/jpc-internal-jof-bottom.png)

get.description: retrieve account information by accountAddress. The type of value in {accountAddress} is defined by accountSchema

parameters.name: accountAddress
parameters.description: Account Address of the user

parameters.name: accountSchema
parameters.description: Schema of the account address of the user. Defined as 'accountId' if using accountId, or 'IBAN' in case of using Iban ..etc

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Authorization header in which the token is placed

parameters.name: x-interactions-id
parameters.description: Unique id for the interaction, used in all request and responses that are related

parameters.name: x-idempotency-key
parameters.description: Unique id that is generated for each request and response. Used for tracking and to check for duplicate calls

parameters.name: x-financial-id
parameters.description: Unique id for the Financial Institution; BIC or Pseudo-BIC code of the sender

parameters.name: x-jws-signature
parameters.description: JWS Signature, refer to security reference

parameters.name: x-auth-date
parameters.description: Date and time of the last authentication of the User by the TPP

parameters.name: x-customer-id
parameters.description: Unique id for the User

parameters.name: x-customer-ip-address
parameters.description: The User's IP address if the user is currently logged in with the TPP

parameters.name: x-customer-user-agent
parameters.description: Indicates the user-agent the User is using. Helps protecting from comprimised devices

get.description: retrieve list of accounts with information

parameters.name: accountStatus
parameters.description: filter by status of account

parameters.name: accountType
parameters.description: filter by type of account. Defined as {main type}.{sub type}.{sub type}.. etc where types are seperated by a "."

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: The number of resources to skip showing in this response. Used for pagination

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of resources to return in the response. Used for pagination

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sorting the response by creation date of the resources (ascending or descending)

parameters.name: Authorization
parameters.description: Authorization header in which the token is placed

parameters.name: x-interactions-id
parameters.description: Unique id for the interaction, used in all request and responses that are related

parameters.name: x-idempotency-key
parameters.description: Unique id that is generated for each request and response. Used for tracking and to check for duplicate calls

parameters.name: x-financial-id
parameters.description: Unique id for the Financial Institution; BIC or Pseudo-BIC code of the sender

parameters.name: x-jws-signature
parameters.description: JWS Signature, refer to security reference

parameters.name: x-auth-date
parameters.description: Date and time of the last authentication of the User by the TPP

parameters.name: x-customer-id
parameters.description: Unique id for the User

parameters.name: x-customer-ip-address
parameters.description: The User's IP address if the user is currently logged in with the TPP

parameters.name: x-customer-user-agent
parameters.description: Indicates the user-agent the User is using. Helps protecting from comprimised devices

balancePosition.description: indicates the position of the balance, if its either credit or debit

desc.example: The requested resource could not be found
The requested endpoint is not valid. Please check the url and try again
The requested service is currently unavailable
The service is temporarily down for maintenance. Please try again later
High-level description of the error

errors.description: List of specific errors. Used to display multiple errors if more than one exist

desc.description: Descriptive message of the specific error
desc.example: Specific description of this error object

id.description: Unique identifier for the error response. Used for logging and tracking errors

last.description: links to the last page of resource

next.description: links to the next page of resource

prev.description: links to the previous page of resource

desc.example: The requested content cannot be served in the specified format
The requested format is not supported. Please specify a different format
The rate limit for requests has been exceeded
The maximum number of requests '500/min' has been reached. Try again in '53 seconds'

feeCategory.description: describes the fee if it's the 'default' fee which applies to all institutions, or if it's a 'special' fee that applies only to specific institutions

feeType.description: type of fee, such as surcharges, comissions, stamps.. etc

service.description: the specific service for which the fee applies. A "." is used to split the sub-services

serviceChannel.description: the channel through which the service is provided

desc.example: An internal server error occurred
Error accessing the database. Please contact the development support team or try again later
Insufficient privileges. Access to the requested resource is forbidden
You do not have the necessary permissions to access this resource

identification.description: Identification of the debtor/creditor as seen on the transaction

name.description: Name of the debtor/creditor as seen on the transaction

postalAddress.description: Address of the debtor/creditor as seen on the transaction

desc.example: Invalid request parameters
Required header x-interactions-id is missing
Invalid authentication credentials
Authentication header is missing. Please include the 'Authorization' header in your request.
The requested HTTP method is not allowed for this resource
PATCH method is not supported for this resource

code.description: account type code. defined as {main type}.{sub type}.{sub type}.. etc where types are seperated by a "."

desc.example: The requested media encoding is not supported
The requested format 'application/xml' is not supported
